refined_query.search_strategy: This refined search targets papers exploring the theoretical and numerical aspects of quantum tunneling, including its applications and related phenomena like decoherence, using relevant keywords for academic database searches.

papers.title: Quantum tunneling of composite object coupled with quantized radiation field
papers.abstract: We study quantum tunneling of a composite object, which has a dipole or quadrupole moment coupled with quantized (photon or gravitational) radiation field, through a δ potential barrier. The dipole or quadrupole moment is represented by an oscillator in the relative coordinate of two constituent particles of the object. The center of mass degrees of freedom of the object is not directly coupled with the radiation field. However, we show that, for the object with the oscillator in the excited state, dissipation caused by the radiation field can suppress its quantum tunneling rate in the center of mass degrees of freedom. In addition, when the initial energy of its center of mass motion is similar to that of the excited state of the oscillator, a spatial superposition state of the object prepared by the barrier can decohere due to the radiation field. The main purpose of this article is to investigate how two different interplays (i) among the center of mass, the relative coordinate degrees of freedom and the potential barrier, and (ii) between the relative coordinate degrees of freedom and the radiation field, can affect the quantum tunneling and the creation of the spatial superposition state of the object. Our toy model can give insight into tests of quantum tunneling and quantum superposition of atoms or molecules with its dipole or quadrupole moment coupled with the radiation field.

papers.title: Tianyan: Cloud services with quantum advantage
papers.abstract: Tianyan Quantum Cloud Platform offers cloud services demonstrating quantum advantage capabilities with a Zuchongzhi 3.0-like superconducting quantum processor. This cloud-accessible superconducting quantum prototype, named Tianyan-287, features 105 qubits and achieves high operational fidelities, with single-qubit gates, two-qubit gates, and readout fidelity at 99.90%, 99.56%, 98.7%, respectively. For a specific benchmark task involving random circuit sampling on a 74-qubit system over 24 cycles, the platform completes one million samples in just 18.4 minutes. In contrast, state-of-the-art classical supercomputers would require approximately 16,000 years to complete the equivalent calculation. To facilitate this, the platform provides access via Cqlib, an open-source SDK designed for working with quantum systems at the level of extended quantum circuits, operators, and primitives. The cloud service aims to democratize access to high-performance quantum hardware, enabling the community to validate and explore practical quantum advantages.

papers.title: Quantum tunneling of a singular potential
papers.abstract: Singularity of the potential function makes quantum tunneling problem mathematically underdetermined. To circumvent the difficulties it introduced in physics, a potential singularity cutoff is often used, followed by a reverse limit transition, or is a suitable self-adjoint extension of the Hamiltonian along the entire coordinate axis made. However, both of them somehow affect the singular nature of the problem, and so I discuss here how quantum tunneling will behave if the original singular nature of the Schrodinger equation left untuched. To do this, I use the property of the probability density current that the singularities are mutually destroyed in it. It is found that the mildly singular potential has a finite, but unusual tunneling transparency, in particular, a non-zero value at zero energy of incident particle. The tunneling of one dimensional Coulomb potential exhibits infinitely fast and complete oscillation at the zero energy boundary and a suppresion to zero in the high-energy limit. In the more singular region, the tunneling becomes forbidden, theby repeating the well-known result of the regularized counterparts.

papers.title: Room temperature observation of electron resonant tunneling through InAs/AlAs quantum dots
papers.abstract: Molecular beam epitaxy is employed to manufacture self-assembled InAs/AlAs quantum-dot resonant tunneling diodes. Resonant tunneling current is superimposed on the thermal current, and they make up the total electron transport in devices. Steps in current-voltage characteristics and peaks in capacitance-voltage characteristics are explained as electron resonant tunneling via quantum dots at 77K or 300K, and this is the first time that resonant tunneling is observed at room temperature in III-V quantum-dot materials. Hysteresis loops in the curves are attributed to hot electron injection/emission process of quantum dots, which indicates the concomitant charging/discharging effect.

papers.title: Single-particle quantum tunneling in ionic traps
papers.abstract: We describe a proposal to probe the quantum tunneling mechanism of an individual ion trapped in a double-well electromagnetic potential. The time-evolution of the probability of fluorescence measurement of the electronic ground state is employed to characterize the single-particle tunneling mechanism. The proposed scheme can be used to implement quantum information devices.

papers.title: Theoretical analysis of current–voltage characteristics of GaN/AlN resonant tunneling diodes including electron phase relaxation in quantum well
papers.abstract: The current–voltage (I–V) characteristics of GaN/AlN resonant tunneling diodes (RTDs) including the electron phase relaxation in the quantum well are calculated to investigate the potential peak-to-valley current ratio (PVCR). The calculation results show double peak structures in the I–V characteristics, which are derived from three- and two-dimensional (3D–2D) and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents. They are changed by the phase coherence length for electrons that is influenced by the probability of electron phase relaxation or electron scattering in the quantum well. The PVCRs due to 3D–2D and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents are estimated to be approximately 10 and 2 when the electron phase relaxation is very fast. The peak characteristics due to 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents are sharper than those due to 3D–2D resonant tunneling currents, although the PVCRs due to 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents are weakened by the valley and off-resonant currents of 3D–2D resonant tunneling currents.

papers.title: Quantum Antenna Behavioral Analysis Using Quantum Tunneling
papers.abstract: This paper explores the impact of quantum tunneling on quantum antenna directivity. Quantum tunneling, where particles traverse barriers classically considered insurmountable, is crucial in technologies such as semiconductor devices and nuclear fusion. This paper investigates how tunneling influences antenna performance using theoretical analysis and practical simulations with the QuTiP library in a Jupyter Notebook. The results reveal the significant effects of quantum tunneling on directivity, emphasizing the importance of quantum principles in designing advanced communication systems. This research paves the way for innovations in quantum antenna technology.

papers.title: Simulation Analysis of Factors Affecting the Quantum Tunneling Effect in 1D Double Potential Barriers Structures
papers.abstract: The working principle of a large number of modern scientific equipment has been applied to the quantum tunneling effect. Although the explanations for the relevant principles of this effect are clarified, the calculation process is very complex and difficult to understand. Previous studies combine specific applications without theoretical model references. This study adopts the method of using the time-independent Schrodinger equation to solve the wave function, which is more convenient to obtain the expression of transmission probability. Then, one discusses the relationship between transmission probability and potential barrier width, potential barrier spacing, and incident particle energy. The experimental results show that the transmission probability decreases exponentially from 1 to 0 as the potential barrier width gradually increases. As the potential barrier width increases, the transmission probability exhibits a clear periodic oscillation relationship, the oscillation period remains unchanged, and this paper derives the relationship between the oscillation period and the potential barrier width. As the incident energy of particles gradually increases, the transmission probability exhibits periodic changes, and the minimum value of transmission probability increases with the periodic changes. Based on the analysis, the smaller the potential barrier width, the greater the incident energy, and the more likely quantum tunneling occurs.

papers.title: Theoretical Analysis of Resonant Tunneling Enhanced Field Emission
papers.abstract: In this paper, we develop an exact analytical quantum theory for ﬁeld emission from surfaces with a nearby quantum well, by solving the one-dimensional time-independent Schrödinger equation. The quantum well, which may be introduced by ions, atoms, nanoparticles, etc., is simpliﬁed as a square potential well with depth H , width d , and distance to the surface L . The theory is used to analyze the eﬀects of the quantum well ( d , H , and L ), the cathode properties (work function W and Fermi energy E F ), and dc ﬁeld F . It is found that the quantum well can lead to resonant tunneling enhanced ﬁeld emission up to several orders of magnitude larger than that from bare cathode surfaces. In the meantime, the electron-emission-energy spectrum is signiﬁcantly narrowed. The strong enhancement region is bounded by the conditions eFL + H ≥ W + C and eFL ≤ W , with e being the elementary charge (positive) and C a constant dependent on dc ﬁeld F . It is also found that the linear shift of resonance peaks in the electron-emission-energy spectrum with dc ﬁeld F follows ε p = ε p 0 − eFL , with ε p 0 being approximately the eigenenergies for electrons conﬁned in a square potential well without a dc ﬁeld. The theory provides insights for the design of high-eﬃciency ﬁeld emitters, which can produce a high current and highly collimated electron beams.

cited_from.quote: when the initial energy of its center of mass motion is similar to that of the excited state of the oscillator, a spatial superposition state of the object prepared by the barrier can decohere due to the radiation field

claims.claim: The model investigates the impact of various interplays between the center of mass, relative coordinate degrees of freedom, the potential barrier, and the radiation field on quantum tunneling and spatial superposition.
claims.evidence: "The main purpose of this article is to investigate how two different interplays (i) among the center of mass, the relative coordinate degrees of freedom and the potential barrier, and (ii) between the relative coordinate degrees of freedom and the radiation field, can affect the quantum tunneling and the creation of the spatial superposition state of the object."

cited_from.quote: The main purpose of this article is to investigate how two different interplays (i) among the center of mass, the relative coordinate degrees of freedom and the potential barrier, and (ii) between the relative coordinate degrees of freedom and the radiation field, can affect the quantum tunneling and the creation of the spatial superposition state of the object.

contributions.contribution: Provides a theoretical framework for understanding quantum tunneling and spatial superposition in composite objects coupled with quantized radiation fields.
contributions.evidence: "Our toy model can give insight into tests of quantum tunneling and quantum superposition of atoms or molecules with its dipole or quadrupole moment coupled with the radiation field."

cited_from.quote: Our toy model can give insight into tests of quantum tunneling and quantum superposition of atoms or molecules with its dipole or quadrupole moment coupled with the radiation field.

methodology.approach: The paper utilizes a theoretical model to investigate quantum tunneling of a composite object with a dipole or quadrupole moment coupled to a quantized radiation field through a delta potential barrier.
methodology.evidence: "We study quantum tunneling of a composite object, which has a dipole or quadrupole moment coupled with quantized (photon or gravitational) radiation field, through a δ potential barrier."

cited_from.quote: We study quantum tunneling of a composite object, which has a dipole or quadrupole moment coupled with quantized (photon or gravitational) radiation field, through a δ potential barrier.

limitations: The model is a 'toy model,' suggesting a simplified representation of complex physical systems.
It focuses on specific coupling mechanisms (dipole/quadrupole and radiation field) and may not generalize to all composite objects or radiation fields.

properties.title: Tianyan: Cloud services with quantum advantage
properties.abstract: Tianyan Quantum Cloud Platform offers cloud services demonstrating quantum advantage capabilities with a Zuchongzhi 3.0-like superconducting quantum processor. This cloud-accessible superconducting quantum prototype, named Tianyan-287, features 105 qubits and achieves high operational fidelities, with single-qubit gates, two-qubit gates, and readout fidelity at 99.90%, 99.56%, 98.7%, respectively. For a specific benchmark task involving random circuit sampling on a 74-qubit system over 24 cycles

key_findings.finding: The Tianyan-287 platform, featuring 105 qubits, achieves high operational fidelities: single-qubit gates at 99.90%, two-bit gates at 99.56%, and readout fidelity at 98.7%.
key_findings.evidence: "Tianyan-287…features 105 qubits and achieves high operational fidelities, with single-qubit gates, two-bit gates, and readout fidelity at 99.90%, 99.56%, 98.7%, respectively."

cited_from.quote: Tianyan-287…features 105 qubits and achieves high operational fidelities, with single-qubit gates, two-bit gates, and readout fidelity at 99.90%, 99.56%, 98.7%, respectively.

key_findings.finding: The platform completes one million samples of a random circuit sampling benchmark in 18.4 minutes.
key_findings.evidence: "For a specific benchmark task involving random circuit sampling on a 74-qubit system over 24 cycles, the platform completes one million samples in just 18.4 minutes."

cited_from.quote: For a specific benchmark task involving random circuit sampling on a 74-qubit system over 24 cycles, the platform completes one million samples in just 18.4 minutes.

key_findings.finding: This is a significant speedup compared to state-of-the-art classical supercomputers, which would require approximately 16,000 years to complete the same calculation.
key_findings.evidence: "In contrast, state-of-the-art classical supercomputers would require approximately 16,000 years to complete the equivalent calculation."

cited_from.quote: In contrast, state-of-the-art classical supercomputers would require approximately 16,000 years to complete the equivalent calculation.

claims.claim: The Tianyan Quantum Cloud Platform demonstrates quantum advantage through a benchmark task.
claims.evidence: "Tianyan Quantum Cloud Platform offers cloud services demonstrating quantum advantage capabilities..."

cited_from.quote: Tianyan Quantum Cloud Platform offers cloud services demonstrating quantum advantage capabilities...

contributions.contribution: Providing access to a superconducting quantum processor via a cloud platform.
contributions.evidence: "The cloud service aims to democratize access to high-performance quantum hardware, enabling the community to validate and explore practical quantum advantages."

cited_from.quote: The cloud service aims to democratize access to high-performance quantum hardware, enabling the community to validate and explore practical quantum advantages.

contributions.contribution: Offering a Cqlib SDK for working with quantum systems.
contributions.evidence: "To facilitate this, the platform provides access via Cqlib, an open-source SDK designed for working with quantum systems at the level of extended quantum circuits, operators, and primitives."

cited_from.quote: To facilitate this, the platform provides access via Cqlib, an open-source SDK designed for working with quantum systems at the level of extended quantum circuits, operators, and primitives.

methodology.approach: The research demonstrates quantum advantage by executing a random circuit sampling benchmark on the Tianyan-287 platform and comparing its performance to that of state-of-the-art classical supercomputers.
methodology.evidence: "Tianyan Quantum Cloud Platform offers cloud services demonstrating quantum advantage capabilities with a Zuchongzhi 3.0-like superconducting quantum processor."

cited_from.quote: Tianyan Quantum Cloud Platform offers cloud services demonstrating quantum advantage capabilities with a Zuchongzhi 3.0-like superconducting quantum processor.

limitations: The analysis is based on a single benchmark task; further evaluation with diverse tasks is needed.
The specific details of the classical supercomputer used for comparison are not provided, potentially impacting the accuracy of the speedup calculation.

properties.title: Quantum tunneling of a singular potential
properties.abstract: Singularity of the potential function makes quantum tunneling problem mathematically underdetermined. To circumvent the difficulties it introduced in physics, a potential singularity cutoff is often used, followed by a reverse limit transition, or is a suitable self-adjoint extension of the Hamiltonian along the entire coordinate axis made. However, both of them somehow affect the singular nature of the problem, and so I discuss here how quantum tunneling will behave if the original singular nat

key_findings.finding: A mildly singular potential exhibits a finite, but unusual tunneling transparency, with a non-zero value at zero energy of incident particle.
key_findings.evidence: The tunneling of one dimensional Coulomb potential exhibits infinitely fast and complete oscillation at the zero energy boundary and a suppression to zero in the high-energy limit.

cited_from.quote: The tunneling of one dimensional Coulomb potential exhibits infinitely fast and complete oscillation at the zero energy boundary and a suppression to zero in the high-energy limit.

key_findings.finding: In the more singular region, tunneling becomes forbidden, mirroring the results of regularized counterparts.
key_findings.evidence: theby repeating the well-known result of the regularized counterparts.

cited_from.quote: theby repeating the well-known result of the regularized counterparts.

claims.claim: The potential singularity makes quantum tunneling problem mathematically underdetermined.
claims.evidence: Singularity of the potential function makes quantum tunneling problem mathematically underdetermined.

cited_from.quote: Singularity of the potential function makes quantum tunneling problem mathematically underdetermined.

contributions.contribution: A novel approach to analyzing quantum tunneling in singular potentials by leveraging the properties of the probability density current.
contributions.evidence: I use the property of the probability density current that the singularities are mutually destroyed in it.

cited_from.quote: I use the property of the probability density current that the singularities are mutually destroyed in it.

methodology.approach: The analysis utilizes the property of the probability density current to investigate the behavior of quantum tunneling in singular potentials.
methodology.evidence: It is found that the singularities are mutually destroyed in it.

cited_from.quote: It is found that the singularities are mutually destroyed in it.

limitations: The analysis is limited to one-dimensional systems and specific potential shapes.
The focus is on understanding the behavior of tunneling in singular potentials, without explicitly addressing the mathematical underdetermination issue.

properties.title: Room temperature observation of electron resonant tunneling through InAs/AlAs quantum dots
properties.abstract: Molecular beam epitaxy is employed to manufacture self-assembled InAs/AlAs quantum-dot resonant tunneling diodes. Resonant tunneling current is superimposed on the thermal current, and they make up the total electron transport in devices. Steps in current-voltage characteristics and peaks in capacitance-voltage characteristics are explained as electron resonant tunneling via quantum dots at 77K or 300K, and this is the first time that resonant tunneling is observed at room temperature in III-V q

properties.title: Single-particle quantum tunneling in ionic traps
properties.abstract: We describe a proposal to probe the quantum tunneling mechanism of an individual ion trapped in a double-well electromagnetic potential. The time-evolution of the probability of fluorescence measurement of the electronic ground state is employed to characterize the single-particle tunneling mechanism. The proposed scheme can be used to implement quantum information devices.

key_findings.finding: The proposed scheme can be utilized to investigate the quantum tunneling of an individual ion.
key_findings.evidence: "The proposed scheme can be used to implement quantum information devices."

cited_from.quote: The proposed scheme can be used to implement quantum information devices.

claims.claim: It is possible to probe the quantum tunneling mechanism of an ion trapped in a double-well electromagnetic potential.
claims.evidence: "We describe a proposal to probe the quantum tunneling mechanism of an individual ion trapped in a double-well electromagnetic potential."

cited_from.quote: We describe a proposal to probe the quantum tunneling mechanism of an individual ion trapped in a double-well electromagnetic potential.

contributions.contribution: A novel scheme for characterizing quantum tunneling using fluorescence measurements of an individual ion in a double-well potential.
contributions.evidence: "The proposed scheme can be used to implement quantum information devices."

cited_from.quote: The proposed scheme can be used to implement quantum information devices.

methodology.approach: The research proposes using the time-evolution of fluorescence probability to characterize the tunneling mechanism of a single ion in a double-well potential.
methodology.evidence: "The time-evolution of the probability of fluorescence measurement of the electronic ground state is employed to characterize the single-particle tunneling mechanism."

cited_from.quote: The time-evolution of the probability of fluorescence measurement of the electronic ground state is employed to characterize the single-particle tunneling mechanism.

limitations: The paper does not detail the specific experimental setup or parameters.
The feasibility of implementing quantum information devices based on this scheme is not fully explored.

properties.title: Theoretical analysis of current–voltage characteristics of GaN/AlN resonant tunneling diodes including electron phase relaxation in quantum well
properties.abstract: The current–voltage (I–V) characteristics of GaN/AlN resonant tunneling diodes (RTDs) including the electron phase relaxation in the quantum well are calculated to investigate the potential peak-to-valley current ratio (PVCR). The calculation results show double peak structures in the I–V characteristics, which are derived from three- and two-dimensional (3D–2D) and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents. They are changed by the phase coherence length for electrons that is influenced by the probabili

key_findings.finding: Double peak structures are observed in the I–V characteristics, arising from 3D–2D and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents.
key_findings.evidence: The calculation results show double peak structures in the I–V characteristics, which are derived from three- and two-dimensional (3D–2D) and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents.

cited_from.quote: The calculation results show double peak structures in the I–V characteristics, which are derived from three- and two-dimensional (3D–2D) and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents.

key_findings.finding: PVCRs are approximately 10 and 2 for 3D–2D and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents when electron phase relaxation is very fast.
key_findings.evidence: The PVCRs due to 3D–2D and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents are estimated to be approximately 10 and 2 when the electron phase relaxation is very fast.

cited_from.quote: The PVCRs due to 3D–2D and 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents are estimated to be approximately 10 and 2 when the electron phase relaxation is very fast.

key_findings.finding: 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents produce sharper peaks compared to 3D–2D resonant tunneling currents.
key_findings.evidence: The peak characteristics due to 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents are sharper than those due to 3D–2D resonant tunneling currents

cited_from.quote: The peak characteristics due to 2D–2D resonant tunneling currents are sharper than those due to 3D–2D resonant tunneling currents

claims.claim: The PVCR is influenced by the phase coherence length, which is affected by electron phase relaxation or scattering.
claims.evidence: They are changed by the phase coherence length for electrons that is influenced by the probability of electron phase relaxation or electron scattering in the quantum well.

cited_from.quote: They are changed by the phase coherence length for electrons that is influenced by the probability of electron phase relaxation or electron scattering in the quantum well.

contributions.contribution: Theoretical analysis of the I-V characteristics of GaN/AlN RTDs considering electron phase relaxation.
contributions.evidence: The current–voltage (I–V) characteristics of GaN/AlN resonant tunneling diodes (RTDs) including the electron phase relaxation in the quantum well are calculated to investigate the potential peak-to-valley current ratio (PVCR).

cited_from.quote: The current–voltage (I–V) characteristics of GaN/AlN resonant tunneling diodes (RTDs) including the electron phase relaxation in the quantum well are calculated to investigate the potential peak-to-valley current ratio (PVCR).

methodology.approach: Theoretical calculation of current–voltage (I–V) characteristics of GaN/AlN RTDs, focusing on resonant tunneling currents and their dependence on electron phase relaxation.
methodology.evidence: The current–voltage (I–V) characteristics of GaN/AlN resonant tunneling diodes (RTDs) including the electron phase relaxation in the quantum well are calculated to investigate the potential peak-to-valley current ratio (PVCR).

cited_from.quote: The current–voltage (I–V) characteristics of GaN/AlN resonant tunneling diodes (RTDs) including the electron phase relaxation in the quantum well are calculated to investigate the potential peak-to-valley current ratio (PVCR).

limitations: The analysis is theoretical and does not include experimental validation.
The specific parameters governing electron phase relaxation (e.g., relaxation time) are not explicitly defined.

properties.title: Quantum Antenna Behavioral Analysis Using Quantum Tunneling
properties.abstract: This paper explores the impact of quantum tunneling on quantum antenna directivity. Quantum tunneling, where particles traverse barriers classically considered insurmountable, is crucial in technologies such as semiconductor devices and nuclear fusion. This paper investigates how tunneling influences antenna performance using theoretical analysis and practical simulations with the QuTiP library in a Jupyter Notebook. The results reveal the significant effects of quantum tunneling on directivity,

key_findings.finding: Quantum tunneling significantly affects antenna directivity.
key_findings.evidence: The results reveal the significant effects of quantum tunneling on directivity, emphasizing the importance of quantum principles in designing advanced communication systems.

cited_from.quote: The results reveal the significant effects of quantum tunneling on directivity, emphasizing the importance of quantum principles in designing advanced communication systems.

claims.claim: Quantum tunneling is a crucial phenomenon for technologies like semiconductor devices and nuclear fusion.
claims.evidence: Quantum tunneling, where particles traverse barriers classically considered insurmountable, is crucial in technologies such as semiconductor devices and nuclear fusion.

cited_from.quote: Quantum tunneling, where particles traverse barriers classically considered insurmountable, is crucial in technologies such as semiconductor devices and nuclear fusion.

contributions.contribution: Investigation into the influence of quantum tunneling on antenna directivity.
contributions.evidence: This research paves the way for innovations in quantum antenna technology.

cited_from.quote: This research paves the way for innovations in quantum antenna technology.

methodology.approach: Theoretical analysis combined with practical simulations using the QuTiP library in a Jupyter Notebook.
methodology.evidence: This paper investigates how tunneling influences antenna performance using theoretical analysis and practical simulations with the QuTiP library in a Jupyter Notebook.

cited_from.quote: This paper investigates how tunneling influences antenna performance using theoretical analysis and practical simulations with the QuTiP library in a Jupyter Notebook.

limitations: The paper does not detail the specific antenna design or simulation parameters used.

properties.title: Simulation Analysis of Factors Affecting the Quantum Tunneling Effect in 1D Double Potential Barriers Structures
properties.abstract: The working principle of a large number of modern scientific equipment has been applied to the quantum tunneling effect. Although the explanations for the relevant principles of this effect are clarified, the calculation process is very complex and difficult to understand. Previous studies combine specific applications without theoretical model references. This study adopts the method of using the time-independent Schrodinger equation to solve the wave function, which is more convenient to obtai

properties.title: Theoretical Analysis of Resonant Tunneling Enhanced Field Emission
properties.abstract: In this paper, we develop an exact analytical quantum theory for ﬁeld emission from surfaces with a nearby quantum well, by solving the one-dimensional time-independent Schrödinger equation. The quantum well, which may be introduced by ions, atoms, nanoparticles, etc., is simpliﬁed as a square potential well with depth H , width d , and distance to the surface L . The theory is used to analyze the eﬀects of the quantum well ( d , H , and L ), the cathode properties (work function W and Fermi ene
